config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society functions best when individual rights are protected first, because once the state or majority can override them too easily, abuse becomes very likely. In my view, the common good matters, but it should be pursued through fair rules that do not sacrifice basic freedom, dignity, and due process. From my perspective, prioritizing society over the individual too much often ends up hurting ordinary people the most.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan daripada hak individu, karena menurut pengalaman dan nilai yang banyak saya lihat di Amerika Serikat, aturan mayoritas tanpa batas justru mudah berubah menjadi penindasan. Hak individu seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum yang adil, dan perlindungan dari kekuasaan sewenang-wenang bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; itu justru fondasi agar masyarakat tetap sah dan tidak semena-mena. Saya setuju bahwa ada situasi darurat di mana kepentingan publik perlu dibatasi sementara, tetapi pembatasan itu harus ketat, proporsional, dan bisa diawasi, bukan dijadikan alasan untuk mengorbankan orang per orang. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikalahkan atas nama “masyarakat,” yang paling sering dirugikan justru kelompok yang paling lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: From my perspective in Indonesia, the statement is too broad and dangerous: society’s interests should not automatically take priority over individual rights. I have seen how easily “for the public good” can be used to excuse abuse, silence criticism, or pressure minorities, especially when power is uneven. The common good matters, but it has to be built on firm protections for basic rights, because without that, “society” often just means the loudest or strongest group. In emergencies, some temporary limits may be necessary, but they must be narrow, accountable, and never treated as a blank check.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena di Amerika Serikat saya melihat hak individu sebagai pagar utama terhadap penyalahgunaan kekuasaan oleh mayoritas maupun negara. Argumen tentang kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi terlalu sering dipakai sebagai dalih untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau mengorbankan due process. Ketika hak dasar mudah dikalahkan atas nama “demi semua orang,” yang biasanya paling menderita justru orang-orang yang paling lemah. Kepentingan publik harus dicapai lewat pembatasan yang sempit, sementara, dan bisa diawasi, bukan dengan menjadikan individu sebagai korban demi abstraksi bernama masyarakat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because in Indonesia that logic is too easy to abuse by officials, majorities, or even local pressure groups. When people say “for the common good,” it often sounds noble, but in practice it can become a cover for silencing criticism, sidelining minorities, or ignoring due process. I do think the public interest matters, especially in emergencies, but it should be pursued through narrow and accountable limits, not by treating individual rights as disposable. A society is not truly healthy if it protects its image or convenience by sacrificing the dignity and freedom of its own people.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu, karena dari perspektif Amerika Serikat, itu terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran bagi kekuasaan mayoritas atau negara yang berlebihan. Poin tentang “kepentingan publik” memang penting, tetapi seperti yang sudah disebut, istilah itu sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau mengabaikan proses hukum yang adil. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak dasar yang kuat, lalu pembatasan hanya boleh dilakukan secara sempit, sementara, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, yang disebut “demi masyarakat” sering kali hanya berarti mengorbankan orang yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
